content.text: Contract Test Integration in CI Pipelines

Consumer-driven contract tests catch compatibility breaks between GenAI service consumers and providers — but only if they run on every commit and gate every deployment. A contract test that lives in a local developer folder and never runs in CI is a documentation artifact, not a safety net.

This sub-subtopic covers the two-pipeline model: how the consumer service CI publishes pacts, how the provider service CI fetches and verifies them, and how the shared Pact Broker (or Pactflow) mediates the can-i-deploy gate that blocks unsafe deployments.

attrs.alt: Contract test CI pipeline: consumer publishes pacts, provider verifies, broker gates deploys

content.text: The Two-Pipeline Model

Contract testing involves two independent CI pipelines that communicate through the Pact Broker. Neither pipeline has to wait for the other to finish before running — the broker keeps the shared state.
Consumer CI: runs contract tests, publishes the generated pact JSON, then checks can-i-deploy before shipping.
Provider CI: triggered by its own push or by a pact webhook from the broker, fetches all consumer pacts, verifies them against the live service, publishes results.
Pact Broker: central store of pacts, verification results, and deployment records — the source of truth for can-i-deploy.
Consumer Pipeline: Publish Pacts on Every PR

The consumer pipeline generates pact files during its test run and publishes them to the broker. The consumer version label is the git commit SHA so the broker can link pacts to specific code versions.

# .github/workflows/consumer-ci.yml
name: Consumer CI

on: [push, pull_request]

env:
  PACT_BROKER_URL: ${{ secrets.PACT_BROKER_URL }}
  PACT_BROKER_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.PACT_BROKER_TOKEN }}
  CONSUMER_VERSION: ${{ github.sha }}

jobs:
  contract-tests:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pip install -r requirements.txt pact-python

      - name: Run consumer contract tests
        run: pytest tests/contracts/consumer/ -v -m contract
        # Pact library writes pact/*.json during this step

      - name: Publish pacts to broker
        run: |
          pact-broker publish pacts/ \
            --consumer-app-version "$CONSUMER_VERSION" \
            --tag "${{ github.ref_name }}" \
            --broker-base-url "$PACT_BROKER_URL" \
            --broker-token "$PACT_BROKER_TOKEN"

  can-i-deploy:
    needs: contract-tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Check can-i-deploy (staging)
        run: |
          pact-broker can-i-deploy \
            --pacticipant ChatbotFrontend \
            --version "$CONSUMER_VERSION" \
            --to-environment staging \
            --broker-base-url "$PACT_BROKER_URL" \
            --broker-token "$PACT_BROKER_TOKEN"

  deploy-staging:
    needs: can-i-deploy
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Deploy to staging
        run: ./scripts/deploy.sh staging

      - name: Record deployment
        run: |
          pact-broker record-deployment \
            --pacticipant ChatbotFrontend \
            --version "$CONSUMER_VERSION" \
            --environment staging \
            --broker-base-url "$PACT_BROKER_URL" \
            --broker-token "$PACT_BROKER_TOKEN"
Provider Pipeline: Verify Pacts on Every Push
The provider pipeline fetches the pacts the broker has for this provider and runs them against the real running service. The key flags —

marks.type: — write results back so can-i-deploy can read them.

content.text:
# tests/contracts/provider/test_provider_verify.py
import pytest
import os
from pact import Verifier

PACT_BROKER_URL = os.environ["PACT_BROKER_URL"]
PROVIDER_VERSION = os.environ.get("PROVIDER_VERSION", "local")
PROVIDER_BASE_URL = os.environ.get("PROVIDER_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:8000")


@pytest.fixture(scope="session", autouse=True)
def start_provider():
    """Start the FastAPI server in test mode before verification."""
    import subprocess, time
    proc = subprocess.Popen(
        ["uvicorn", "app.main:app", "--port", "8000", "--env-file", ".env.test"]
    )
    time.sleep(2)
    yield
    proc.terminate()


def test_provider_pacts():
    verifier = Verifier(
        provider="GenAIService",
        provider_base_url=PROVIDER_BASE_URL,
    )
    success, _ = verifier.verify_with_broker(
        broker_url=PACT_BROKER_URL,
        broker_token=os.environ["PACT_BROKER_TOKEN"],
        provider_version=PROVIDER_VERSION,
        provider_version_tags=[os.environ.get("GIT_BRANCH", "main")],
        publish_verification_results=True,
        # Only verify pacts from consumers deployed to prod (safe default)
        consumer_version_selectors=[
            {"deployedOrReleased": True},
            {"branch": "main"},
        ],
    )
    assert success == 0, "Provider failed to verify one or more consumer pacts"

# .github/workflows/provider-ci.yml
name: Provider CI

on:
  push:
    branches: ["main", "feature/**"]
  # Pactflow webhook triggers this workflow when a new pact is published
  repository_dispatch:
    types: [pact-changed]

env:
  PACT_BROKER_URL: ${{ secrets.PACT_BROKER_URL }}
  PACT_BROKER_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.PACT_BROKER_TOKEN }}
  PROVIDER_VERSION: ${{ github.sha }}

jobs:
  verify-pacts:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install & start provider
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          uvicorn app.main:app --port 8000 &
          sleep 3

      - name: Verify consumer pacts
        env:
          GIT_BRANCH: ${{ github.ref_name }}
        run: pytest tests/contracts/provider/ -v

  can-i-deploy:
    needs: verify-pacts
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: can-i-deploy (prod)
        run: |
          pact-broker can-i-deploy \
            --pacticipant GenAIService \
            --version "$PROVIDER_VERSION" \
            --to-environment production \
            --broker-base-url "$PACT_BROKER_URL" \
            --broker-token "$PACT_BROKER_TOKEN"
Pact Broker Webhooks: Trigger Provider CI from Consumer Pact Changes

Without webhooks, the provider only verifies pacts on its own push. A consumer could ship a new pact at 9 AM and wait all day for the provider to push something. Webhooks close this gap — the broker notifies the provider CI whenever a pact changes.
Provider State Endpoints in CI
Provider state handlers let the verifier set up database or cache state before each interaction. In CI the provider must expose a

marks.type: endpoint that accepts POST requests and applies the state.

content.text:
# app/pact_routes.py  (only mounted in TEST / CI environments)
from fastapi import APIRouter, Request
from app.database import db

pact_router = APIRouter(prefix="/_pact")


@pact_router.post("/provider_states")
async def set_provider_state(request: Request):
    body = await request.json()
    state = body.get("state", "")
    params = body.get("params", {})

    if state == "the embedding model is available":
        # ensure model mock is healthy
        pass

    elif state == "the vector index has 50 documents":
        db.clear_vectors()
        db.seed_vectors(count=50)

    elif state == "the LLM rate limit is exceeded":
        # patch the LLM client to return 429 responses
        from app import llm_client
        llm_client.force_rate_limit = True

    elif state == "the model context limit is exceeded":
        from app import llm_client
        llm_client.force_context_exceeded = True

    return {"state": state, "applied": True}


# Mount only in test environments
# In main.py:
#   if os.getenv("ENV") in ("test", "ci"):
#       app.include_router(pact_router)
Common Pitfalls in CI Contract Test Integration
Pitfall
Symptom
Fix
Not publishing verification results
can-i-deploy always returns 'no results' and blocks deploy
Set publish_verification_results=True in verifier; pass --broker-token
Using 'main' as the consumer version label
Broker can't track which code was deployed; can-i-deploy is unreliable
Use git SHA (${{ github.sha }}) as the version label in every publish call
Skipping record-deployment
can-i-deploy shows 'not deployed anywhere' and allows bad deploys
Call pact-broker record-deployment after every successful deploy, for every environment
Provider state endpoint mounted in production
State mutations (db.clear()) affect real data
Guard with if os.getenv('ENV') in ('test', 'ci') before including the router
Verifying all pacts instead of deployed ones
Old broken pacts from stale branches block the pipeline
Use consumer_version_selectors: [{deployedOrReleased: True}, {branch: 'main'}]
Selective Pact Verification with Consumer Version Selectors

By default, the verifier fetches all pacts ever published — including stale development branches. Consumer version selectors narrow what the provider verifies, eliminating noise from abandoned branches while still covering every consumer that is actually deployed.

# Selector patterns for the verifier (passed to verify_with_broker)

# Option 1 — safest for most teams
consumer_version_selectors = [
    {"deployedOrReleased": True},   # any consumer currently in any env
    {"branch": "main"},             # the consumer's main branch (for pre-deploy checks)
]

# Option 2 — include the consumer's current PR branch so provider
# catches breaks before the consumer merges
consumer_version_selectors = [
    {"deployedOrReleased": True},
    {"branch": "main"},
    {"branch": os.environ.get("CONSUMER_FEATURE_BRANCH", "")},  # passed via webhook payload
]

# Option 3 — verify only what's in production (lowest noise, highest risk of missing pre-prod breaks)
consumer_version_selectors = [
    {"environment": "production"},
]

# Anti-pattern — verifies everything, including dead branches
# consumer_version_selectors = [{"all": True}]  # noisy, avoid

verifier.verify_with_broker(
    broker_url=PACT_BROKER_URL,
    broker_token=os.environ["PACT_BROKER_TOKEN"],
    provider_version=os.environ["PROVIDER_VERSION"],
    consumer_version_selectors=consumer_version_selectors,
    publish_verification_results=True,
)
Testing the CI Pipeline Locally with a Docker Pact Broker

Running the full two-pipeline integration locally lets developers debug webhook configuration, verify pact publishing, and test can-i-deploy without polluting a shared broker. Docker Compose is the fastest path.

# docker-compose.pact.yml — local Pact Broker for development
version: "3.9"

services:
  pact-broker:
    image: pactfoundation/pact-broker:latest
    ports:
      - "9292:9292"
    environment:
      PACT_BROKER_DATABASE_ADAPTER: sqlite
      PACT_BROKER_DATABASE_NAME: /tmp/pact_broker.sqlite3
      PACT_BROKER_BASIC_AUTH_USERNAME: admin
      PACT_BROKER_BASIC_AUTH_PASSWORD: admin
      PACT_BROKER_ALLOW_PUBLIC_READ: "true"
    volumes:
      - pact_broker_data:/tmp

volumes:
  pact_broker_data:

# Start the local broker
docker compose -f docker-compose.pact.yml up -d

# Run consumer tests against local broker
PACT_BROKER_URL=http://localhost:9292 \
PACT_BROKER_TOKEN=admin:admin \
CONSUMER_VERSION=$(git rev-parse HEAD) \
pytest tests/contracts/consumer/ -v

# Publish to local broker
pact-broker publish pacts/ \
  --consumer-app-version $(git rev-parse HEAD) \
  --tag $(git branch --show-current) \
  --broker-base-url http://localhost:9292 \
  --broker-username admin --broker-password admin

# Check can-i-deploy against local broker (will fail — no provider has verified yet)
pact-broker can-i-deploy \
  --pacticipant ChatbotFrontend \
  --version $(git rev-parse HEAD) \
  --to-environment staging \
  --broker-base-url http://localhost:9292 \
  --broker-username admin --broker-password admin
Monitoring Contract Test Health in CI

Contract test results are only useful if someone looks at them. Add monitoring that alerts when verification results are not published within a time window, indicating the provider pipeline is broken.

# scripts/check_pact_health.py
# Run in a scheduled CI job (e.g. daily)
import os, requests, sys
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone

BROKER_URL = os.environ["PACT_BROKER_URL"]
BROKER_TOKEN = os.environ["PACT_BROKER_TOKEN"]
STALE_HOURS = 24  # alert if no verification in this window

headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {BROKER_TOKEN}"}
r = requests.get(f"{BROKER_URL}/pacts/latest", headers=headers, timeout=10)
r.raise_for_status()

problems = []
for pact in r.json().get("_embedded", {}).get("pacts", []):
    name = pact.get("_embedded", {}).get("consumer", {}).get("name", "?")
    last_verified = pact.get("_embedded", {}).get("latestVerificationResults", {}).get("verifiedAt")
    if last_verified is None:
        problems.append(f"{name}: never verified")
        continue
    age = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - datetime.fromisoformat(last_verified.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
    if age > timedelta(hours=STALE_HOURS):
        problems.append(f"{name}: last verified {age.total_seconds()/3600:.1f}h ago (threshold {STALE_HOURS}h)")

if problems:
    print("PACT HEALTH FAILURES:")
    for p in problems:
        print(f"  - {p}")
    sys.exit(1)

print(f"OK: all {len(r.json().get('_embedded', {}).get('pacts', []))} pacts verified within {STALE_HOURS}h")
Summary

Contract test CI integration follows a two-pipeline model: the consumer publishes pact files on every push; the provider fetches and verifies them and publishes results back to the broker. Neither can deploy without passing

marks.type: , which queries the broker for a verified result between the specific consumer and provider versions targeting a specific environment.

content.text: Key operational details: use the git SHA as the version label, always call

marks.type: after a successful deploy, use webhooks so the provider verifies new pacts immediately rather than waiting for its own push, and protect provider state endpoints so they only run in CI.
